suppress.notes: FALSE POSITIVE (verified 2026-06-11; pending Security-Lead confirmation). CVE-2026-42154 is an unauthenticated DoS in the PROMETHEUS SERVER's remote-read endpoint (/api/v1/read) — the Go project github.com/prometheus/prometheus, fixed in Prometheus 3.5.3 / 3.11.3 (ref: nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42154). This service depends on io.micrometer:micrometer-registry-prometheus, the Java metrics-EXPOSITION client that serves /actuator/prometheus for scraping. It implements NO remote-read endpoint and contains none of the vulnerable Prometheus-server code. dependency-check mis-attributes the server CVE to the similarly-named client via fuzzy CPE matching; no micrometer release (incl. latest 1.17.0) will ever "fix" a Prometheus-server CVE. Scoped to this package + CVE only.

FALSE POSITIVE — mis-assigned CPE (verified 2026-06-15; pending Security-Lead confirmation). dependency-check assigns the generic SDK CPE cpe:/a:opentelemetry:opentelemetry to the JAVA artifact io.opentelemetry.semconv:opentelemetry-semconv, so every OpenTelemetry-GO CVE matches this jar via fuzzy CPE matching. The Java semconv jar is GENERATED SEMANTIC-CONVENTION CONSTANTS ONLY (attribute-name strings) — it has no runtime/executable code, no command execution, and no HTTP client, so none of these Go-SDK CVEs can apply. It is pulled transitively via micrometer-tracing-bridge-otel / opentelemetry-exporter-otlp. Confirmed Go-only CVEs currently mis-attributed (all in go.opentelemetry.io/otel, fixed in opentelemetry-go v1.43.0): - CVE-2026-24051 — PATH hijacking / arbitrary code execution (Darwin ioreg, BSD kenv); GHSA-9h8m-3fm2-qjrq - CVE-2026-39882 — OTLP-HTTP exporter unbounded-body DoS / memory exhaustion; GHSA-w8rr-5gcm-pp58 - CVE-2026-39883 — BSD/Solaris kenv PATH-hijacking follow-up; GHSA-hfvc-g4fc-pqhx Suppressing the mis-assigned CPE (not per-CVE) so future OpenTelemetry-Go CVEs do not re-trigger against this constants-only Java jar. Scoped to this package + CPE only; genuine Java CVEs (matched by the jar's own maven coordinates) remain unaffected.
